title: PulseStep: force-mode emit + ring-buffer trail for always-visible tracing
desc: PulseStep.Init/Line/Done emit to stderr gated by WKAPPBOT_PROFILE env — use .Line and .Finish (force=true) for always-visible tracing independent of env. Ring buffer GetRecentTrail(n) captures last 20 emitted lines; used to dump recent context in UAC/elevation prompts.
steps:
WHEN TO USE WHICH METHOD
  PulseStep.Init(label)     — opens session, prints only if WKAPPBOT_PROFILE=1
  PulseStep.Mark(label)     — threshold-gated (<100ms delta = silent)
  PulseStep.Line(label)     — FORCE mode, always prints (force=true)
  PulseStep.Done(label)     — closes session, threshold-gated
  PulseStep.Finish(label)   — FORCE close, always prints
For always-on tracing (e.g. --sudo flow), use Line + Finish.

FORCE MODE VS WKAPPBOT_PROFILE
Emit(msg, force=false) path:
  if (force

_profEnabled) { Console.Error.WriteLine(msg); RecordTrail(msg); }
Where _profEnabled = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("WKAPPBOT_PROFILE") == "1".
So Init/Mark/Done are silent in prod unless env set.
Line/Finish pass force=true → always emit AND record to ring buffer.

RING BUFFER (v5.14)
PulseStep maintains LinkedList _trail (size 20).
Every Emit call adds to trail (even throttled ones that didn't print? — actually only when force

_profEnabled).
Query: PulseStep.GetRecentTrail(int n = 5) → IReadOnlyList string (newest last).
Use case: dump recent pulse lines before UAC / on error / in crash log.

GOTCHA — STDERR VISIBILITY UNDER LAUNCHER
Output goes to Console.Error.
Launcher IocpPipeRelay default = stderr buffered (invisible).
For visibility: run with --stderr flag (or --sudo since v5.14.x auto-enables).
See: skill iocp-stderr-default-buffered

NESTING + ASYNC
Each session has its own depth via AsyncLocal _nestDepth.
Printed with 2-space indent per level.
GC finalizer warns if Init called without Done/Finish (leaked session).

FILES
  csharp/src/WKAppBot.CLI/StepProfiler.cs — implementation
  csharp/src/WKAppBot.CLI/Program.cs:233+ — --sudo usage
  csharp/src/WKAppBot.CLI/Commands/ElevatedEyeProxy.cs — trail consumer
author: claude
